# Overview [CURRENT] ‚Äî package: overview ‚Äî rendered: 2026-04-13T22:04:27Z

The orchestration model is the "outside" of the agent system ‚Äî the entry points and control surfaces that the controller skill uses to submit goals, monitor progress, handle blocked states, and resolve permissions/interrupts. While the agent-execution view describes the *inside* of an agent call and graph-nodes describes the *data*, this view describes the services and REST controllers that expose and manage the running workflow.

The single most important class in this view is `LlmOrchestrationController` (`/api/ui/`), which is the primary controller the controller skill interacts with for workflow monitoring and goal management.

# Goal Lifecycle [CURRENT] ‚Äî package: goal-lifecycle ‚Äî rendered: 2026-04-13T22:04:27Z

References: 5 files tracked (package: goal-lifecycle) ‚Äî see `$.sections["Goal Lifecycle"]` in .metadata/HEAD

A goal is submitted via the controller skill and flows through these services:

```
POST /api/ui/goals/start  (LlmOrchestrationController)
 ‚îÇ  or  POST /api/ui/quick-actions { actionType: "START_GOAL" }
 ‚îÇ
 ‚ñº
LlmOrchestrationController.startGoal()
 ‚îî‚îÄ Delegates to OrchestrationController.startGoalAsync()
      ‚îú‚îÄ Creates ArtifactKey (root nodeId)
      ‚îú‚îÄ Publishes GoalStartedEvent
      ‚îî‚îÄ Delegates to GoalExecutor.executeGoal() (@Async ‚Äî fire-and-forget)
           ‚îú‚îÄ Normalizes repository URL (must be local filesystem path, not GitHub URL)
           ‚îú‚îÄ Resolves repo path on disk
           ‚îî‚îÄ Calls AgentLifecycleHandler.initializeOrchestrator()
                ‚îú‚îÄ Creates OrchestratorNode via ComputationGraphOrchestrator
                ‚îú‚îÄ Creates MainWorktreeContext via WorktreeService ‚Üí GitWorktreeService
                ‚îî‚îÄ Starts the Embabel @Agent workflow
```

After `GoalExecutor` fires, the REST layer has no direct handle on the running workflow. All subsequent monitoring happens through polling and event endpoints.

# How `workflowGraph()` builds the response [CURRENT] ‚Äî package: how-workflowgraph-builds-the-response ‚Äî rendered: 2026-04-13T22:04:27Z

The workflow-graph polling flow is central to the controller loop:

```
workflowGraph(nodeId, errorWindowSeconds)
 ‚îÇ
 ‚îú‚îÄ toRootNodeId(nodeId) ‚Äî walks ArtifactKey.parent() to root
 ‚îú‚îÄ graphRepository.findSubtree(rootNodeId) ‚Äî all nodes in the tree
 ‚îú‚îÄ eventStreamRepository.computeScopedStats(rootNodeId) ‚Äî global stats
 ‚îú‚îÄ For each node: eventStreamRepository.computeMetrics(nid) ‚Äî per-node metrics
 ‚îÇ
 ‚îú‚îÄ buildWorkflowNode() recursively:
 ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ For each child node:
 ‚îÇ    ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ AskPermissionNode ‚Üí PendingItem(type="PERMISSION")
 ‚îÇ    ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ InterruptNode ‚Üí PendingItem(type="INTERRUPT")
 ‚îÇ    ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ ReviewNode ‚Üí PendingItem(type="REVIEW")
 ‚îÇ    ‚îÇ    ‚îî‚îÄ other ‚Üí recurse
 ‚îÇ    ‚îú‚îÄ propagationItemService.findPendingBySourceNodeId(nid) ‚Üí PendingItem(type="PROPAGATION")
 ‚îÇ    ‚îî‚îÄ WorkflowNodeMetrics with pendingItems list
 ‚îÇ
 ‚îî‚îÄ controllerEndpointTransformationIntegration.maybeTransform() ‚Üí final response
```

The `pendingItems` array is what the controller skill uses to detect blocked states and decide which resolution endpoint to call.

# Dependencies [CURRENT] ‚Äî package: dependencies ‚Äî rendered: 2026-04-13T22:04:27Z

`LlmOrchestrationController` depends on:
- `GraphRepository` ‚Äî node tree lookups
- `EventStreamRepository` ‚Äî event storage and metrics
- `CliEventFormatter` ‚Äî formats events into human-readable text with truncation
- `UiStateStore` ‚Äî server-side immutable state tree (same reducer as TUI)
- `PropagationItemService` ‚Äî pending propagation items per node
- `LayerIdResolver` ‚Äî resolves controller-level filter layer ID
- `OrchestrationController` ‚Äî goal submission delegation
- `ControllerEndpointTransformationIntegration` ‚Äî post-response transformation policies

# Interrupt & Permission Handling [CURRENT] ‚Äî package: interrupt-permission ‚Äî rendered: 2026-04-13T22:04:27Z

References: 7 files tracked (package: interrupt-permission) ‚Äî see `$.sections["Interrupt & Permission Handling"]` in .metadata/HEAD

When agents need human input or tool permissions, the workflow blocks and the controller resolves:

# Permissions (`/api/permissions`) [CURRENT] ‚Äî package: permissions-api-permissions ‚Äî rendered: 2026-04-13T22:04:27Z

```
Agent calls a tool ‚Üí ACP sandbox requires permission
 ‚îÇ
 ‚ñº
PermissionGate (Kotlin) blocks the agent thread
 ‚îú‚îÄ Records pending permission request with requestId, nodeId, toolCallId
 ‚îî‚îÄ Waits for resolution
      ‚îÇ
      ‚ñº
Controller polls:  GET /api/permissions/pending
 ‚îî‚îÄ Returns List<PendingPermissionSummary> with requestId, nodeId, permissions details
      ‚îÇ
      ‚ñº
Controller resolves:  POST /api/permissions/resolve
 ‚îî‚îÄ PermissionResolutionRequest { id, optionType: ALLOW_ONCE | ALLOW_ALWAYS | REJECT_ONCE | REJECT_ALWAYS, note }
      ‚îî‚îÄ PermissionGate unblocks the agent thread with the resolution
```

`PermissionGateService` provides detail lookups and scope-based resolution (can resolve all permissions under an `ArtifactKey` subtree).

# Interrupts (`/api/interrupts`) [CURRENT] ‚Äî package: interrupts-api-interrupts ‚Äî rendered: 2026-04-13T22:04:27Z

```
Agent routing returns InterruptRequest
 ‚îÇ
 ‚ñº
WorkflowGraphService.handleRoutingInterrupt()
 ‚îú‚îÄ Creates InterruptContext on origin node (WAITING_INPUT / WAITING_REVIEW)
 ‚îî‚îÄ Creates InterruptNode or ReviewNode as child
      ‚îÇ
      ‚ñº
Controller polls:  GET /api/interrupts/pending
 ‚îî‚îÄ Returns List<PendingInterruptSummary> with interruptId, originNodeId, reason, type
      ‚îÇ
      ‚ñº
Controller resolves:  POST /api/interrupts/resolve
 ‚îî‚îÄ InterruptService.handleInterrupt() ‚Äî for AGENT_REVIEW runs a separate LLM call
      using the review_resolution template; for HUMAN_REVIEW unblocks on user input
```

Only `HUMAN_REVIEW` interrupt type is accepted via the REST API ‚Äî the controller acts as the human for routing decisions.

# Agent Conversations [CURRENT] ‚Äî package: agent-conversations ‚Äî rendered: 2026-04-13T22:04:27Z

References: 7 files tracked (package: agent-conversations) ‚Äî see `$.sections["Agent Conversations"]` in .metadata/HEAD

Structured conversations between agents and the controller follow a full decoration pipeline on both sides of the exchange:

```
Agent LLM calls call_controller MCP tool (AgentTopologyTools)
 ‚îÇ
 ‚îú‚îÄ Validates sessionId and justificationMessage
 ‚îú‚îÄ Resolves callingNode and callingType from graph
 ‚îú‚îÄ Resolves OperationContext from the agent's Embabel blackboard
 ‚îú‚îÄ Creates AgentToControllerRequest (contextId = callingKey.createChild())
 ‚îÇ
 ‚îî‚îÄ Delegates to AgentExecutor.controllerExecution():
      ‚îÇ
      ‚îú‚îÄ 1. Decorates request (RequestResultsDecorator pipeline)
      ‚îú‚îÄ 2. Builds PromptContext (factory + decorator chain)
      ‚îú‚îÄ 3. Assembles prompt (Jinja template + prompt contributors)
      ‚îú‚îÄ 3b. Runs LlmCallDecorators for side effects (e.g. PromptReceivedEvent)
      ‚îÇ   NOTE: No LLM call ‚Äî the rendered prompt IS the agent's message
      ‚îî‚îÄ 4. interruptService.publishAndAwaitControllerInterrupt()
              ‚îú‚îÄ Sets InterruptContext(WAITING_INPUT) on origin node
              ‚îî‚îÄ Blocks agent thread via PermissionGate
```

The controller side uses the same pipeline in reverse:

```
Controller polls:  POST /api/agent-conversations/list
 ‚îî‚îÄ Returns pending conversations (interruptId, originNodeId, agentType, message)
      ‚îÇ
      ‚ñº
Controller responds:  POST /api/agent-conversations/respond
 ‚îî‚îÄ AgentConversationController delegates to AgentExecutor.controllerResponseExecution():
      ‚îÇ
      ‚îú‚îÄ 1. Derives key hierarchy: interruptKey ‚Üí contextId (child), agentChatId (parent)
      ‚îú‚îÄ 2. Resolves OperationContext from origin node
      ‚îú‚îÄ 3. Retrieves original AgentToControllerRequest from blackboard as lastRequest
      ‚îú‚îÄ 4. Builds ControllerToAgentRequest with correct key hierarchy
      ‚îú‚îÄ 5. Decorates request + builds PromptContext + assembles prompt
      ‚îú‚îÄ 6. Runs LlmCallDecorators for side effects
      ‚îú‚îÄ 7. permissionGate.resolveInterrupt() ‚Üí unblocks the agent thread
      ‚îî‚îÄ 8. Emits AgentCallEvent(RETURNED) for observability
```

Both directions use the Jinja template system ‚Äî `communication/controller_call.jinja` for the agent‚Üícontroller leg, `communication/controller_response.jinja` for controller‚Üíagent. The prompt contributor chain fires in both directions, applying the same context enrichment (git state, topology, we-are-here). This means the rendered text the controller sees and the rendered text the agent receives are both fully decorated, not raw strings.

`ActivityCheckController` (`POST /api/ui/activity-check`) provides a lightweight polling endpoint that checks for any pending state (permissions, interrupts, conversations) across all active sessions ‚Äî the controller uses this to avoid polling each endpoint separately.

# Computation Graph Orchestrator [CURRENT] ‚Äî package: computation-graph ‚Äî rendered: 2026-04-13T22:04:27Z

References: 4 files tracked (package: computation-graph) ‚Äî see `$.sections["Computation Graph Orchestrator"]` in .metadata/HEAD

`ComputationGraphOrchestrator` is the **node management service** (not the workflow engine). It provides:

- **Node CRUD**: `getNode()`, `getAllNodes()`, `getChildNodes()`, `addChildNodeAndEmitEvent()`
- **Event emission**: Every mutation emits events (`NodeAddedEvent`, `NodeStatusChangedEvent`, `NodeErrorEvent`, `InterruptResolved`)
- **Parent-child linking**: `addChildNodeAndEmitEvent()` calls `updateNodeChildren()` ‚Äî an exhaustive switch over all `GraphNode` types ‚Äî to add the child ID to the parent's list

The actual workflow traversal (which phase to enter, when to fan out, how to route) lives in `WorkflowGraphService` and `WorkflowGraphState`. These track the routing table mapping phase roles to node IDs, and `WorkflowGraphService` handles phase transitions, interrupt routing, and fan-out dispatch.

# Worktree Creation [CURRENT] ‚Äî package: worktree-creation ‚Äî rendered: 2026-04-13T22:04:27Z

References: 2 files tracked (package: worktree-creation) ‚Äî see `$.sections["Worktree Creation"]` in .metadata/HEAD

Git worktrees provide filesystem isolation for parallel agent execution. Only the initial creation path is actively used:

```
GoalExecutor
 ‚îî‚îÄ WorktreeService.createMainWorktree(repoUrl, baseBranch, derivedBranch, nodeId)
      ‚îî‚îÄ GitWorktreeService creates branch + worktree on disk
           ‚îú‚îÄ Detects submodules ‚Üí creates SubmoduleWorktreeContext for each
           ‚îî‚îÄ Returns MainWorktreeContext (worktreeId, path, branch, submodules)
```

`WorktreeService` also provides `attachWorktreesTo[Phase]Requests()` which copies the worktree context onto each dispatched request during fan-out.

# Communication Topology [CURRENT] ‚Äî package: communication-topology ‚Äî rendered: 2026-04-13T22:04:27Z

References: 3 files tracked (package: communication-topology) ‚Äî see `$.sections["Communication Topology"]` in .metadata/HEAD

The topology system controls which agents can talk to which:

```
CommunicationTopologyConfig (YAML-driven)
 ‚îî‚îÄ defines allowed communication pairs per AgentType
      ‚îÇ
      ‚ñº
CommunicationTopologyProvider
 ‚îî‚îÄ runtime queries: canCommunicate(sourceType, targetType)
      ‚îÇ
      ‚ñº
AgentCommunicationService
 ‚îú‚îÄ listAvailableAgents(callingSessionKey, callingAgentType)
 ‚îÇ    Filters by: topology-permitted AND session-open AND not-self
 ‚îÇ    Returns AgentAvailabilityEntry (key, type, busy, callable, reachableVia)
 ‚îî‚îÄ validateCall(callingType, targetType)
```

The `reachableVia` field enables indirect routing ‚Äî if agent A can't call B directly, it reports which intermediaries can reach B.

# Propagation Items [CURRENT] ‚Äî package: propagation-items ‚Äî rendered: 2026-04-13T22:04:27Z

References: 3 files tracked (package: propagation-items) ‚Äî see `$.sections["Propagation Items"]` in .metadata/HEAD

The controller skill monitors propagation items as a key signal for agent decisions:

```
Controller polls:  GET /api/propagations/items
 ‚îî‚îÄ PropagationItemService returns all pending items
      ‚îÇ
      ‚ñº
Controller polls:  POST /api/propagations/items/by-node
 ‚îî‚îÄ Filtered by node scope ‚Äî used in poll.py alongside workflow-graph
      ‚îÇ
      ‚ñº
PropagationController exposes the REST surface for propagation queries
```

Propagators are the escalatory mechanism for out-of-domain signals. When a propagator fires, the item appears in the workflow graph's `pendingItems` as type `PROPAGATION`.

# Session & Control [CURRENT] ‚Äî package: session-control ‚Äî rendered: 2026-04-13T22:04:27Z

References: 3 files tracked (package: session-control) ‚Äî see `$.sections["Session & Control"]` in .metadata/HEAD

`SessionKeyResolutionService` resolves which ACP session to use for a given agent call:

1. Check if the request carries an explicit `sessionArtifactKey`
2. If not, derive from the `DecoratorContext.operationContext` (Embabel blackboard identity)
3. Map to `AcpSessionRoutingKey` which `AcpSessionManager` uses to find/create the session

Each agent gets its own isolated ACP session ‚Äî the session key is derived from the graph node identity, not from the agent type.

`AgentControlService` provides pause/stop/resume operations, used by the interrupt controller to suspend agent execution when review is requested.

# Skill Cross-Reference [CURRENT] ‚Äî package: skill-cross-reference ‚Äî rendered: 2026-04-13T22:04:27Z

The controller session depends on five skills that bridge Java source and Python/markdown operational knowledge. Changes to Java code often require corresponding skill updates and vice versa ‚Äî this section is the cross-reference.

**multi_agent_ide_controller** ‚Äî The primary skill. Contains: `executables/poll.py` (workflow polling), `executables/interrupts.py` (interrupt resolution), `executables/permissions.py` (permission resolution), `executables/conversations.py` (agent conversation handling), `executables/ack_propagations.py` (propagation acknowledgement), `executables/search_tool_calls.py` (tool call search). Workflows: `standard_workflow.md` (full controller loop). References: `program_model.md`, `session_identity_model.md`, `prompt_architecture.md`, `we_are_here_prompt.md`, `filter_policy_contracts.md`, `propagator_contracts.md`.

**multi_agent_ide_deploy** ‚Äî Clone/sync/deploy. `scripts/clone_or_pull.py` (3-phase: clone/sync, verification gate, provision). `scripts/deploy_restart.py` (restart with health check, profiles: claude, claudellama, codex). Branch management: `create-feature-branch.py`, `sync-feature-branch.py`, `merge-feature-to-main.py`, `pull-merged-code.py`. Always deploy from tmp repo, never from source checkout.

**multi_agent_ide_api** ‚Äî Schema-first API interaction. `scripts/api_schema.py` queries live OpenAPI spec at levels 1-4. Always use level 4 when constructing request bodies. Covers all REST endpoints: goal submission, workflow graph, permissions, interrupts, propagators, filters.

**multi_agent_ide_debug** ‚Äî Log searching and error triage. `executables/error_search.py` for structured error search (summary + detail modes). `executables/error_patterns.csv` is the institutional memory ‚Äî add new patterns when discovered. Exception knowledge base: `references/ignore-exceptions.md` (confirmed benign), `references/common-exceptions.md` (recurring issues and workarounds).

**multi_agent_ide_validation** ‚Äî Test execution and analysis. Tensor decomposition: SURFACE.md -> INVARIANTS.md -> EXPLORATION.md. `tests-multi-agent-ide-validation.sh` (~30 min full pipeline). `workflows/validation_workflow.md` is the canonical validation process.

**multi_agent_ide_contracts** ‚Äî Internal type contracts not in OpenAPI. Filter instruction schemas, resolution enums, propagation types, serialized shapes. Validate against source before use.

# Workflow-Graph Polling (LlmOrchestrationController) [CURRENT] ‚Äî package: workflow-graph-polling ‚Äî rendered: 2026-04-13T22:04:27Z

`LlmOrchestrationController` (`@RequestMapping("/api/ui")`) is the primary controller the controller skill polls. It provides these endpoints:

| Endpoint | What it does |
|---|---|
| `POST /workflow-graph` | **Primary polling endpoint.** Returns the full node tree rooted at the given nodeId, with per-node metrics (event counts, error counts, token usage) and `pendingItems` that surface PERMISSION, INTERRUPT, REVIEW, and PROPAGATION blocked states. Response may be transformed by `ControllerEndpointTransformationIntegration`. |
| `POST /workflow-graph/status` | Lightweight terminal-status check: RUNNING / COMPLETE / FAILED. |
| `POST /goals/start` | Submit a new goal (delegates to `OrchestrationController`). |
| `POST /quick-actions` | START_GOAL or SEND_MESSAGE ‚Äî convenience endpoint combining goal creation and message injection. |
| `POST /nodes/events` | Paginated, formatted event summaries for a node scope (node + descendants). Truncation via `CliEventFormatter`. |
| `POST /nodes/events/detail` | Full formatted detail for a single event. Truncation parameters control field length. |
| `POST /nodes/events/stream` | SSE stream for real-time events (via `SseEventAdapter`). |
| `POST /nodes/state` | Current `UiStateSnapshot` for a node scope (via `UiStateStore`). |
| `POST /nodes/actions` | Dispatch a `UiActionCommand` via EventBus. |
